en.extract:
The pygmy flowerpecker (Dicaeum pygmaeum) is a species of bird in the family Dicaeidae.
It is endemic to the Philippines.
Its natural habitats are tropical moist lowland forest and tropical moist montane forest.


Description and taxonomy


Subspecies
Five subspecies are recognized:

D. p.pygmaeum   —  Found on Luzon (except Northwest), Corregidor, Lubang, Polillo Is, Mindoro, Marinduque, Maestre de Campo, Semirara, Sibay, Boracay, Romblon, Sibuyan, Ticao, Masbate, Samar, Gigantes, Calagnaan, Sicogon, Guimaras, Negros, Cebu, Bohol, Siquijor and Leyte
D. p. davao — Found on Camiguin Sur and Mindanao
D. p. palawanorum —   Found on Calauit, Culion, Palawan and Balabac
D. p.salomonseni —  Found on Northwest Luzon
D. p.fugaense —  Found on Calayan and Fuga


Ecology and behavior
Often seen feeding around flowering and fruiting trees and mistletoes. Feeds on fruit, nectar and pollen. Forages in the canopy or high in the understory singly, in small groups or joins mixed-species flocks. 
Barely anything is known about its breeding habits. Fledgling seen in May. Birds in breeding condition with enlarged gonads collected from February to December.


Habitat and conservation status
Its natural habitats at tropical moist lowland primary forest and secondary forest up to 2,000 meters above sea level but mostly seen below 1,000 meters above sea level. 
The IUCN Red List has assessed this bird as least-concern species as it is common throughout its range and is found throughout the entire Philippines. Its population is still said to be decreasing due to deforestation in the Philippines due to land conversion, illegal logging and mining.


References

The '''pygmy flowerpecker''' ('''''Dicaeum pygmaeum''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Dicaeidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Philippines]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[tropical moist lowland forest]] and tropical moist [[montane forest]].

== Description and taxonomy ==
=== Subspecies ===
Five [[subspecies]] are recognized:

* ''D. p.pygmaeum   —''  Found on Luzon (except Northwest), Corregidor, Lubang, Polillo Is, Mindoro, Marinduque, Maestre de Campo, Semirara, Sibay, Boracay, Romblon, Sibuyan, Ticao, Masbate, Samar, Gigantes, Calagnaan, Sicogon, Guimaras, Negros, Cebu, Bohol, Siquijor and Leyte
* ''D. p. davao —'' Found on Camiguin Sur and Mindanao
* ''D. p. palawanorum —''   Found on Calauit, Culion, Palawan and Balabac
* ''D. p.salomonseni —''  Found on Northwest Luzon 
* ''D. p.fugaense —''  Found on Calayan and Fuga<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Cheke |first1=Robert |last2=Mann |first2=Clive |date=2020 |title=Pygmy Flowerpecker (Dicaeum pygmaeum), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/pygflo1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.pygflo1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Ecology and behavior ==
Often seen feeding around flowering and fruiting trees and mistletoes. Feeds on fruit, nectar and pollen. Forages in the canopy or high in the understory singly, in small groups or joins mixed-species flocks. 
Barely anything is known about its breeding habits. Fledgling seen in May. Birds in breeding condition with enlarged gonads collected from February to December.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Cheke |first1=Robert |last2=Mann |first2=Clive |date=2020 |title=Pygmy Flowerpecker (Dicaeum pygmaeum), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/pygflo1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.pygflo1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
Its natural [[Habitat|habitats]] at [[Subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest|tropical moist lowland]] [[primary forest]] and [[secondary forest]] up to 2,000 meters above sea level but mostly seen below 1,000 meters above sea level. 

The [[IUCN Red List]] has assessed this bird as [[least-concern species]] as it is common throughout its range and is found throughout the entire Philippines. Its population is still said to be decreasing due to [[deforestation in the Philippines]] due to land conversion, illegal logging and mining.<ref>{{Cite report |url=http://www.iucnredlist.org/details/22717531/0 |title=Dicaeum pygmaeum: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2016: e.T22717531A94537956 |last=IUCN |date=2016-10-01 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/iucn.uk.2016-3.rlts.t22717531a94537956.en |language=en}}</ref>

zh.extract:
小啄花鸟（学名：Dicaeum pygmaeum），是啄花鸟科啄花鸟属的一种，为菲律宾的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
小啄花鸟的平均体重约为5.0克。栖息于亚热带或热带的湿润低地林。
